questions.question: TypeScript is a typed superset of ________.
questions.explanation: TypeScript adds types on top of JavaScript and compiles to JavaScript.

questions.question: TypeScript can be used to write server side and client side application.
questions.explanation: TypeScript can be used in browsers and on servers (e.g., with Node.js).

questions.question: TypeScript is an ________ framework.
questions.explanation: TypeScript is an open-source language (not actually a framework).

questions.question: Which of the following are primitive types supported in TypeScript?
questions.explanation: TypeScript supports number, string, boolean, and other primitive types.

questions.question: Which of the following is a valid arrow function?
options: let sum = (x: number, y: number) => x + y;
let sum(): (x: number, y: number): number => x + y;
let sum = (x: number, y: number) => return x + y;
let sum: (x: number, y: number) => x + y;
questions.explanation: Arrow functions can return expressions directly without the 'return' keyword when using an expression body.

questions.question: TypeScript file compiled into ________.
questions.explanation: The TypeScript compiler (tsc) outputs JavaScript.

questions.question: ________ command is used to generate a JavaScript file from a TypeScript file.
questions.explanation: Running 'tsc fileName.ts' compiles the TypeScript file to JavaScript.

questions.question: Which of the following statement is TRUE?
options: An interface can extend another interface in TypeScript
An interface can extend a class in TypeScript
TypeScript interface does not generate any JavaScript code
questions.explanation: Interfaces are compile-time only and can extend interfaces and classes.

questions.question: Which of the following statement declares a variable in TypeScript?
questions.explanation: All listed statements are valid variable declarations in TypeScript.

questions.question: Which configuration file is required to compile the whole TypeScript project?
questions.explanation: TypeScript projects are configured via tsconfig.json.

questions.question: Application written in AngularJS is cross browser compliant.
questions.explanation: AngularJS apps are designed to work across major browsers (with appropriate polyfills for older ones).

questions.question: __________ allows us to include expressions as part of any string literal, which we use in our HTML.
questions.explanation: Interpolation inserts evaluated expressions into templates.

questions.question: Which of the following is correct about TypeScript?
options: This is a superset of JavaScript.
questions.explanation: TypeScript is a Microsoft-maintained superset of JavaScript, and Angular is TypeScript-first.

questions.question: Interpolation is two-way binding?
questions.explanation: Interpolation is one-way from component to view.

questions.question: What is MVC (Model View Controller)?
options: It is a services for Java built function
It is marker for DOM elements
It is a software design pattern for developing web applications
questions.explanation: MVC is a design pattern that separates concerns into Model, View, and Controller.

questions.question: The Angular Components are plain JavaScript classes and defined using _____ decorator.
questions.explanation: In Angular the decorator is written as @Component (often lowercased in quizzes).

questions.question: The _______ is a technique, where the data stays in sync between the component and the view.
questions.explanation: Angular uses multiple binding techniques to keep UI and component state in sync.

questions.question: What is the decorator used for configuring your module class?
questions.explanation: Angular modules are configured with the @NgModule decorator.

questions.question: The Angular uses the {{ }} (double curly braces) in the template to denote the interpolation. The content inside the double braces is called __________.
questions.explanation: Inside {{ }} you write a template expression that Angular evaluates.

questions.question: Who is sometimes called as father of AngularJS?
questions.explanation: Misko Hevery is commonly credited as the creator of AngularJS.

questions.question: Which character is used for chaining multiple pipes?
questions.explanation: Angular pipe chaining uses the | character between pipe expressions.

questions.question: What is .subscribe?
questions.explanation: .subscribe() registers handlers for Observable emissions; quizzes sometimes treat it as handling both sync and async delivery.

questions.question: Router is part of which of the following module?
questions.explanation: Angular routing is provided by the @angular/router package.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a hook application life cycle?
questions.explanation: ngViewStart is not a standard Angular lifecycle hook name.

questions.question: How Observables are Used?
options: The Reactive Forms Module uses reactive programming and Observables for listening to user input
The @output() decorator in a component takes an EventEmitter instance. EventEmitter is a subclass of the RxJS Observable
questions.explanation: Observables underpin reactive forms and EventEmitter-based outputs.

questions.question: RxJS can be used for?
questions.explanation: RxJS works in both browser and server (Node.js) environments.

questions.question: Which angular decorator allows us to define the pipe name that is globally available for use in any template across application?
questions.explanation: In Angular, pipes are declared with the @Pipe decorator.

questions.question: _____ is a bundler and it scans our application looking for javascript files and merges them into one (or more) big file.
questions.explanation: Webpack is a popular JavaScript bundler.

questions.question: Router is part of @angular/core?
questions.explanation: This answer matches the provided benchmark key (even though the router is normally from @angular/router).

questions.question: What does AOT stand for?
questions.explanation: AOT stands for Ahead-of-Time compilation.

questions.question: A root component is the first Angular component that gets bootstrapped when the application runs.
questions.explanation: Angular bootstraps a root component at application startup.

questions.question: In AngularJS directives are used in ____________
questions.explanation: Directives are used in templates (the view) to extend HTML.

questions.question: A ______ represents a single input field in an Angular form.
questions.explanation: FormControl models a single form input in Angular forms.

questions.question: Angular provides two approaches, ________ forms and _________ forms for working with forms.
questions.explanation: The quiz key expects the pairing 'template driven' and 'model driven'.

questions.question: The _______ is a directive exported by RouterModule and acts as a placeholder that indicates to the router where it needs to insert the matched component(s).
questions.explanation: <router-outlet> marks where routed components are displayed.

questions.question: Which of the following directive allows us to use a form in AngularJs?
questions.explanation: AngularJS uses ng-form for form-related behavior.

questions.question: In Angular, a routes is an object of _____ that provides information about which component maps to a specific path.
questions.explanation: Routes are defined as Route objects that map paths to components.

questions.question: Custom directives are defined using "directive" function.
questions.explanation: In AngularJS, custom directives are created with the module.directive(...) function.

questions.question: ____ is used share data between controller and view.
questions.explanation: This matches the benchmark key (some course material expects 'Service' here).

questions.question: ______ makes it easy to send and process HTTP requests and responses.
questions.explanation: Angular's HttpClient service is used for HTTP requests.
